reforms.description: Costa Rica plans to finance public research based on competitive and performance-based criteria, aiming to enhance innovation outcomes and interactions between universities and the business sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking funding to performance, the initiative is expected to incentivize higher quality research and foster collaboration with the private sector, ultimately boosting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative is part of the recommendations to improve innovation outcomes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public research is funded, emphasizing performance and competitiveness, which is crucial for enhancing the country's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Finance public research based on competitive and performance-based criteria and establish independent evaluation mechanisms.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is considering changing the way public research is funded by moving towards performance-based and competitive funding, which would increase incentives for universities to enhance the quality and relevance of their research and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the quality and relevance of research can lead to increased innovation and productivity, benefiting the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Costa Rica could consider changing the way public research is funded.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards a more competitive funding model, which could enhance research quality and innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: To trigger stronger interactions, Costa Rica could consider changing the way public research is funded.

reforms.description: Costa Rica has enacted a new law aimed at implementing a centralized register for collecting identity and beneficial ownership information for all legal entities and arrangements, enhancing transparency and compliance with international standards.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving transparency can enhance trust in institutions, which is essential for fostering a conducive environment for innovation and investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a centralized register represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability, which are critical for fostering a robust innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Costa Rica has recently enacted a new law aimed at implementing a centralised register for collecting identity and beneficial ownership information for all legal entities and arrangements.

reforms.description: Costa Rica is working towards reducing administrative burdens and improving the licensing and permits system for starting a business, which currently takes longer than in most OECD countries. The Costa Rican Export Promotion Agency (PROCOMER) has made progress in simplifying and digitalizing business processes, including the registration phase and licenses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing administrative burdens can enhance firm entry and competition, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Authorities are working towards a better legal framework and improving coordination among ministries.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to streamline business processes, which can have a meaningful impact on the ease of doing business and overall economic activity.
reforms.source_quote: Costa Rica is also moving in that direction. The Costa Rican Export Promotion Agency (PROCOMER) has made good progress in simplifying and digitalising all business processes for firms operating in the free trade zones.

reforms.description: The government is enhancing digital governance by launching a web portal, Pura Vida Digital, to facilitate online transactions with government agencies. This initiative aims to improve the efficiency of administrative procedures for firms and citizens.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital governance can reduce transaction costs for businesses and enhance compliance with regulations, fostering a more conducive environment for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Authorities have recently launched a web portal, Pura Vida Digital, which widens the number of transactions with government agencies that can be resolved online.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant step towards modernizing government services and improving accessibility for businesses and citizens.
reforms.source_quote: Authorities have recently launched a web portal, Pura Vida Digital, which widens the number of transactions with government agencies that can be resolved online.

reforms.description: Costa Rica has approved a Competition Reform Act to strengthen its competition framework, addressing weaknesses in the existing system and enhancing the independence and resources of the competition authority, COPROCOM.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to improve competition, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation across the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The Competition Reform Act has been approved and is in the process of implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the competition framework, which is crucial for enhancing market efficiency and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Costa Rica has recently approved a Competition Reform Act, a fundamental legislative step forward to buttress its competition framework and adapt it to best global standards.

reforms.description: Costa Rica's government announced guidelines for state-owned banks to boost financial inclusion, defining loan issuance and intermediation margin targets, and inviting public banks to refinance loans for over-indebted consumers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to credit for households and firms can stimulate investment and consumption, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced guidelines for state-owned banks to boost financial inclusion.
reforms.importance_rationale: The guidelines represent a significant step towards improving financial access, particularly for vulnerable populations, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government of Costa Rica recently announced guidelines for state-owned banks to boost financial inclusion.

reforms.description: The Government of Costa Rica launched the National Financial Education Strategy in January 2019 to enhance citizens' financial literacy and promote better financial decision-making across various demographics.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving financial literacy can lead to better financial decisions, which supports economic stability and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was officially launched and aims to reach various target audiences.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant effort to improve financial literacy, which is crucial for enhancing financial inclusion and economic resilience.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Economy, Industry and Commerce (MEIC) launched the National Financial Education Strategy in January 2019.

reforms.description: The Sistema de Banca para el Desarrollo (SBD) aims to fund SMEs and farmers while providing services to support entrepreneurial activities and innovation, including business training and seed capital financing.
reforms.component_name: SBD Support for SMEs and Innovation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support and training to SMEs, the SBD enhances their capacity for innovation and growth, which can lead to increased productivity and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: SBD provides various services to support innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SBD's initiatives represent a significant effort to enhance the entrepreneurial ecosystem in Costa Rica, particularly for SMEs, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: SBD provides services to its beneficiaries to support entrepreneurial activities and innovation.

reforms.description: Introducing regulatory sandboxes would allow FinTech start-ups in Costa Rica to test their products temporarily, without having to comply with all regulatory requirements, thereby fostering innovation in the sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory burdens, sandboxes can enhance the ability of start-ups to innovate and attract investment, ultimately boosting the FinTech sector's growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal for regulatory sandboxes has been introduced to facilitate FinTech innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of regulatory sandboxes represents a significant shift in the regulatory approach towards FinTech, aiming to enhance innovation and competitiveness in the sector.
reforms.source_quote: Introducing regulatory sandboxes would allow FinTech start-ups in Costa Rica to test their products temporarily, without having to comply with all regulatory requirements.
